tasks.title: SQLAlchemy models + Pydantic schemas for all nine tables
acceptance_criteria: create_all bootstrap against SQLite produces all nine tables
Pydantic schemas (ExtractionField, PoHeaderFields, LineItem, DocumentDetail, ShipToRecord, PromptConfig) match tech_specs signatures field-for-field
no other lane's files are touched

tasks.title: Swappable OCREngine + LLMExtractor protocols, real and fake implementations
acceptance_criteria: fake LLMExtractor simulates the invalid-JSON-then-repair path via a force_repair fixture flag
real and fake implementations both satisfy the same protocol and pass identical interface-conformance tests

tasks.title: Pipeline services wired end-to-end (pdf, validation, orchestration)
acceptance_criteria: fixture PDF run through the fake pipeline produces a DocumentDetail with correct shape and correct needs_review flags
OCR/LLM calls run via run_in_executor/thread pool so the event loop isn't blocked

tasks.title: Ship To fuzzy matching + prompt inheritance service
acceptance_criteria: adversarial near-duplicate ship-to catalog fixtures prove tie-breaking behaves sensibly, not just happy-path
per-customer prompt override correctly supplements/overrides the global prompt with a computable diff

tasks.title: API routes with audit logging built into each mutating endpoint
acceptance_criteria: approveDocument returns 422 when needs_review fields remain and override is false
reprocessDocument rejects clobbering unsynced client edits without an explicit force flag
every mutating endpoint's test asserts exactly one new audit_log row of the correct action type, not just the returned payload

tasks.title: Frontend scaffold, typed API client, and documentReview reducer/context
acceptance_criteria: reducer unit tests pass for every action (FIELD_EDITED, LINE_ITEM_EDITED, LINE_ITEM_ADDED, LINE_ITEM_DELETED, SHIP_TO_SELECTED, REPROCESS_REQUESTED, APPROVE_REQUESTED, RESET_FROM_SERVER) including derived hasUnsavedChanges
api client types are a byte-for-byte mirror of the Pydantic schemas from T-001, never edited independently of them
app builds and runs against static fixture JSON with no live backend required

tasks.title: Shared accessible UI components
acceptance_criteria: ConfidenceBadge always renders a numeric percentage plus an icon, never color-only
PipelineStepper renders a red dot, inline error reason, and stage-scoped retry on failure
components are keyboard-operable and used identically across Dashboard/Upload/Review rather than reimplemented per screen

tasks.title: Dashboard and Upload screens
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows KPI tiles + status-pill document table with empty/loading/success/error states
Upload supports drag-and-drop multi-file batch with a 4-dot per-file pipeline stepper and stage-scoped retry on failure

tasks.title: Document Review screen (core screen)
acceptance_criteria: side-by-side PDF (~55%) + tabbed panel (Extracted Data/Line Items/Raw OCR/Raw JSON) all wired to the reducer from T-006
three field visual states (unreviewed-low-confidence, user-edited, confirmed) all render correctly and never rely on color alone
clicking a field jumps/highlights the matching PDF page; sticky action bar enforces the reprocess-unsaved-edits warning and the approve-override gate

tasks.title: Ship To Catalog, Prompt Configuration, and Settings screens
acceptance_criteria: catalog is a searchable table + slide-over editor with an aliases chip-input
prompt config shows global vs per-customer inherited/diff view and a confirm-gated re-run-affected-documents action
Settings shows API-key/env status without exposing secret values

tasks.title: Docker, docker-compose, README, sample PDFs, env wiring
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up on a clean checkout brings up backend+frontend with no manual steps beyond copying .env.example
no hardcoded secrets anywhere in the images or compose file
README documents the ~10-concurrent-file untested ceiling and the Poppler/GPL dependency note

tasks.title: Final integration smoke test and scripted MVP-success run-through
acceptance_criteria: upload -> OCR -> extract -> edit a low-confidence field -> Ship To match/override -> approve -> export JSON and CSV runs end to end against the real (non-fake) stack
audit_log has a row for every step of that run-through
combined phase-discussion transcript is written to docs/transcripts/fastlane-po.txt per the portfolio build rules
